title: Allow podman start to attach to a running container
body:
If a container is already running, and a user asks to start it -a (attach), we should
honor this and attach to the container.

Signed-off-by: baude <bbaude@redhat.com>

comments.body: If this matches Docker behaviour then lets merge.  Should we update the man page to explain that starting a running container will attach to it?

If you do a create using --tty or --interactive, the `podman start` man page says using the --attach command won't override this? Won't this change over ride that?  I'm not sure what's right, but I think at least the `podman start` man page will need reviewing and/or changes.

@TomSweeneyRedHat You can attach STDIN to a container not created with `--interactive`, but the container itself may not have an STDIN stream (`--interactive` to `podman run` and `podman start` is stored in our config and passed down to Conmon when creating the container, here it just decides whether we try to attach STDIN). The same is true of TTY - the one for `podman run` affects the container config and how we set up conmon, here we just decide whether we need to set raw terminal when attaching.

:pushpin: Commit 1aba400 has been approved by `baude`
